overview.short: Jivdhan (or Jeevdhan) is a hill fortress situated 1 km from modern town of Ghatghar in Junnar Taluka of Pune district in Maharashtra, India.
overview.description: Jivdhan (or Jeevdhan) is a hill fortress situated 1 km from modern town of Ghatghar in Junnar Taluka of Pune district in Maharashtra, India. The fort, which rises 1,145 meters (3,757 feet) above sea level, is located in the Sahyadri mountain range. The fort was looted and destroyed by the British upon siege between 1815 and 1818. Jivdhan is a part of the 'Famous 5' trekking destinations among trekking enthusiasts.

topPlaces.name: Ghatghar
topPlaces.description: Ghatghar is a hill station and small town in the Pune district in the Indian state of Maharashtra. It is a town located at a higher elevation of Deccan Plateau and Naneghat valley.

topPlaces.name: Naneghat
topPlaces.description: Naneghat, also referred to as Nanaghat or Nana Ghat (IAST: Nānāghaṭ), is a mountain pass in the Western Ghats range between the Konkan coast and the ancient town of Junnar in the Deccan plateau. The pass is about 120 kilometres (75 mi) north of Pune and about 165 kilometres (103 mi) east from Mumbai, Maharashtra, India.

topPlaces.name: Chavand, Maharashtra
topPlaces.description: Chavand is a hill fortress situated 30 km from the modern-day town of Naryangoan in Junnar Taluka of Pune district in Maharashtra, India. The fort, which rises 1,036 meters (3,399 feet) above sea level, is located in the Sahyadri mountain range.

items.note: Ghatghar is a hill station and small town in the Pune district in the Indian state of Maharashtra. It is a town located at a higher elevation of Deccan Plateau and Naneghat valley.

Naneghat, also referred to as Nanaghat or Nana Ghat (IAST: Nānāghaṭ), is a mountain pass in the Western Ghats range between the Konkan coast and the ancient town of Junnar in the Deccan plateau. The pass is about 120 kilometres (75 mi) north of Pune and about 165 kilometres (103 mi) east from Mumbai, Maharashtra, India.

Chavand is a hill fortress situated 30 km from the modern-day town of Naryangoan in Junnar Taluka of Pune district in Maharashtra, India. The fort, which rises 1,036 meters (3,399 feet) above sea level, is located in the Sahyadri mountain range.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Jivdhan?
faq.a: The best time to visit Jivdhan is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Pune Airport (81 km) and the nearest railway station is Kalyan Junction (Mumbai) (59 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Jivdhan?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Jivdhan at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Jivdhan?
faq.a: Stays in Jivdhan start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Jivdhan?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ghatghar, Naneghat, Chavand, Maharashtra.
